title: quercus robur var. dshorochensis
intro: Quercus robur var. dshorochensis, also known as the Dshorochensis Oak, is a lesser-known variety of the common English Oak tree (Quercus robur) native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of Quercus robur var. dshorochensis contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable medicinal plant. The health benefits of Quercus robur var. dshorochensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial activities. Quercus robur var. dshorochensis can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and benefits.
